sources.label: OpenNav waypoint database (VAMPI, MEKAR, NILAM, GOLUD, PUKAR, IDKUT)
mh370wiki.net: History of the flight (quotes Factual Information 2015)
mh370wiki.net: Waypoints and MH370 (filed flight plan table from FI 2015)
Gilbert, 'An Analysis of MH370's Flight Path Between Waypoint IGARI...' (PDF; quotes FI 2015 pages 1-2, 7, 46, 54 event times)
Iannello (radiantphysics) / mh370search: 18:22 last primary radar position interpretations

ATC-Caption-2025 (mh370-caption.net): Malaysian civil ATC radar decode - Kota Bharu PSR in/out, Butterworth PSR (corrected), Kota Bharu SSR - CSVs with computed lat/lon from raw rho/theta; fetched and unzipped 2026-07-16 (HTTP 200)

Geoscience Australia / ATSB 'KL-LSTRP' military-radar-derived numeric track (467 points, ~10 s spacing 16:42:27-18:01:49 + single point 18:22:12), republished by mh370-caption.net; fetched 2026-07-16 (HTTP 200)
Iannello, 'The Civilian Radar Data for MH370' (11 Apr 2018) - original range/azimuth release (581 rows KL SSR + KB/BW PSR) that ATC-Caption-2025 supersedes with computed lat/lon
Malaysian Factual Information report (FI, 2015) PDF - verbatim military-radar altitude/speed passages, fetched 2026-07-16 (HTTP 200)
Malaysian Safety Investigation Report (SIR, 2018) PDF mirror - verbatim radar passages pp.50-52, waypoint table p.341, Table 2.2C p.344; fetched 2026-07-16 (HTTP 200)

meta.method: Epoch 1: waypoint/report-quoted positions plus the last actual SSR return from the ATC-Caption-2025 decode. Epoch 2: recorded radar positions - Geoscience/ATSB KL-LSTRP military 10-s track for the IGARI turnback (the only published numeric coordinates of the turn), Kota Bharu PSR and Butterworth PSR (corrected) civil returns decimated from ~4 s scans, then FI-timed anchors across the two acknowledged radar gaps to the 18:22:12 last fix.
notes: Times are UTC on 7 March 2014 (MYT = UTC+8, i.e. local 8 March). Event times are quoted from the Malaysian Factual Information report (2015) and Safety Investigation Report (2018); radar-point times come from the datasets themselves (rounded here to whole seconds; the CSVs carry ~10 ms precision).

Epoch 1 lateral path is modeled as departure runway 32R -> filed waypoints PIBOS, IKUKO -> IGARI (filed-route coordinates from the FI flight plan table on mh370wiki.net). Aircraft was cleared 'direct IGARI' at 16:43; filed waypoints lie within a few NM of the direct track, so the polyline is representative, not radar-derived.

LAST SECONDARY RADAR (updated 2026-07-16): official reports publish TIME ONLY for the 17:21:13 event - SIR Table 2.2C (PDF p.344): '3.2 nm after passing IGARI, SSR radar position symbol of MH370 dropped off' at 1721:13 UTC. The widely circulated coordinate ~6°55'N 103°34.7'E appears in neither FI nor SIR text. The ATC-Caption-2025 decode gives the last ACTUAL Kota Bharu SSR return at 17:20:42 UTC, 6.951783N 103.597754E (Mode A 2157, callsign MAS370); KL ACC's 17:21:13 was the display symbol persisting ~30 s after that last return. The 17:21:13 point here remains dead-reckoned ~42 s past IGARI on the 059 track toward BITOD and is marked interpolated.

EPOCH 2 TURNBACK (updated 2026-07-16): the IGARI turn itself (17:21-17:30) uses the Geoscience Australia / ATSB 'KL-LSTRP' numeric track - the military-radar-derived data Malaysia transmitted to the ATSB (matches the DSTG book description: 10-s intervals 16:42:27-18:01:49 plus a single point at 18:22:12). Its timestamps are estimated labels reconstructed from point spacing by mh370-caption.net ('purely indicative'); positions are the published values. Easternmost point of the turn 17:22:45 at 7.090278N 103.840000E, northernmost 17:24:51 at 7.190278N 103.611668E.

EPOCH 2 PENINSULA CROSSING (updated 2026-07-16): 17:30:33-18:01:21 uses recorded civil primary radar - Kota Bharu PSR inbound (17:30:33-17:36:54), Kota Bharu PSR outbound (17:38:55-17:44:35, after the overhead cone gap), and Butterworth PSR corrected (17:46:21-18:01:21) - decimated from the ~4 s ATC-Caption-2025 scans (281 PSR rows total). lat/lon are computed by the dataset authors from raw rho/theta with radar-head positions given in-file (KB PSR 6.163639N 102.293778E, BW PSR 5.472131N 100.394659E). Primary radar carries no altitude.

RADAR GAPS: (a) Kota Bharu overhead cone 17:36:54-17:38:55; (b) Butterworth data gap ~17:48-17:51:34; (c) the acknowledged long gap - SIR p.50: the military 'blip' disappeared at 1803:09 and reappeared 1815:25-1822:12. Points between 18:01:49 and 18:21:50 other than the FI-timed VAMPI anchor are straight-line interpolations and flagged as such. No numeric coordinates for the 18:15:25-18:22:12 reappearance segment other than the final fix have been published anywhere (the Lido-slide points were an image, never a table).

The 18:22:12 last primary radar fix has two published interpretations ~117 km apart: (a) SIR figure: 10 NM beyond MEKAR on N571 toward NILAM = 6.577655N 96.340864E (used here); (b) Beijing Lido military slide: 200 NM from Butterworth bearing 295T = 6.871123N 97.354047E (not used). The Geoscience/KL-LSTRP numeric track's final row - 6.5998525N 96.3111310E at 18:22:12 - lies ~2.8 km from (a), strongly corroborating the SIR/MEKAR interpretation. SIR text itself (p.50) reads '...until 1822:12 UTC, about 195 nm from Butterworth, on a heading of 285°, speed of 516 kt and at an altitude of 29,500 ft.'

ALTITUDES (verbatim, fetched 2026-07-16): FI p.3: 'At 1730:35 UTC to 1735 UTC the radar return was on heading 231 magnetic (M), ground speed of 496 knots (kt.) and registered height of 35,700 ft.'; FI: 17:36-17:36:40 'height fluctuation between 31,100 and 33,000 ft'; FI: 17:39:59 'height at 32,800 ft'. SIR p.50 additionally reports 44,700 ft south of Penang (17:52:31) and 4,800 ft near Pulau Perak (18:01:59) BUT disclaims all of them: 'the altitude and speed extracted from the data are subjected to inherent error. The only useful information obtained from the Military radar was the latitude and longitude position of the aircraft as this data is reasonably accurate.' Military-derived altitudes are therefore carried only in labels, never as altFt, except the FI cruise values in Epoch 1 and the widely-quoted (equally disclaimed) 29,500 ft at 18:22:12.

The former schematic anchor at Pulau Perak (island coordinate at '18:02') was REMOVED 2026-07-16: it was the island's position, not an aircraft fix, and conflicts with the recorded Butterworth track (5.597N 99.106E at 18:01:21). The island remains a POI; SIR places the military blip near Pulau Perak at 1801:59.

OpenNav MEKAR (6.5039N 96.4911E) and NILAM (6.7564N 95.9764E) were cross-validated: the independently published '10 NM past MEKAR toward NILAM' position 6.577655N 96.340864E lies exactly 10 NM along the MEKAR->NILAM leg built from the OpenNav coordinates.

epoch1.label: Cleared to push back, gate C1 WMKK (airport reference point)
epoch1.source: time: FI p1 via Gilbert PDF; position: OurAirports WMKK

epoch1.label: Takeoff from runway 32R (threshold coordinates, field elev ~70 ft)
epoch1.source: time: FI p1 via Gilbert PDF; position: OurAirports runways.csv WMKK 32R threshold

epoch1.label: Climbing right turn to the northeast (cleared FL180 direct IGARI at 00:43 (UTC+8))
Near filed waypoint PIBOS, climbing (cleared FL350 at 00:50 (UTC+8))
epoch1.source: position: FI filed flight plan (PIBOS N0320.5 E10203.1) via mh370wiki.net; time interpolated

epoch1.label: Crew reports maintaining FL350
epoch1.source: time/alt: FI App 1.18E via Gilbert PDF; position interpolated

epoch1.label: Last ACARS transmission (HDG 027, FL350, Mach 0.821)
epoch1.source: time/alt/hdg: FI p46 via Gilbert PDF; position interpolated

epoch1.label: Near filed waypoint IKUKO
epoch1.source: position: FI filed flight plan (IKUKO N0545.2 E10313.4) via mh370wiki.net; time interpolated

epoch1.label: Final voice transmission: 'Good night, Malaysian three seven zero'
epoch1.source: time: FI App 1.18E via Gilbert PDF; position interpolated

epoch1.label: Passing waypoint IGARI (6°56'12"N 103°35'06"E)
epoch1.source: time: FI p2 via Gilbert PDF; position: Wikipedia MH370 article, cross-checked with FI flight plan table (N0656.2 E10335.1) via mh370wiki.net

epoch1.label: Last actual SSR return (Kota Bharu radar head, Mode A 2157, callsign MAS370); the KL ACC display symbol persisted another ~30 s
epoch1.source: ATC-Caption-2025 KB_SSR_MH370.csv final return 17:20:42.30 (computed lat/lon from raw rho/theta); Kuantan SSR last return 17:20:31.51 at 6.937599N 103.567123E corroborates

epoch1.label: SSR position symbol dropped off KL ATCC display, 3.2 NM past IGARI (Mode S symbol lost 01:20:36 (UTC+8))
epoch1.source: time + '3.2 nm after passing IGARI': SIR Table 2.2C p.344 / FI p2; position interpolated (dead-reckoned ~42 s past IGARI on 059° toward BITOD; official reports publish no coordinate for this event)

epoch2.label: Start of primary-radar-only epoch (same point as last secondary return)
Military-derived track resumes just past IGARI; transponder off, turn beginning
epoch2.source: Geoscience/ATSB KL-LSTRP numeric track (times are indicative labels; position published)

epoch2.label: Easternmost point of the turnback - the aircraft continued ~15 NM toward BITOD before reversing
Northernmost point of the turnback hook, turning left through north onto a southwesterly track
Established southwest-bound back toward the Malay peninsula
Kota Bharu civil primary radar acquires the target. FI: 01:30:35 (UTC+8)-01:35 (UTC+8) heading 231M, ground speed 496 kt, 'registered height of 35,700 ft' (military; altitude disclaimed by SIR)
epoch2.source: ATC-Caption-2025 KB_PSR_IN (recorded, ~4 s scans, decimated); FI p3 verbatim altitude quote

epoch2.label: FI: 01:36 (UTC+8)-01:36:40 (UTC+8) 'height fluctuation between 31,100 and 33,000 ft' (military; disclaimed)
epoch2.source: ATC-Caption-2025 KB_PSR_IN (recorded); FI p3 altitude quote

epoch2.label: Crossing the coast just north of Kota Bharu; last inbound PSR return before the radar's overhead cone (FI coast-crossing time 01:37:22 (UTC+8))
epoch2.source: ATC-Caption-2025 KB_PSR_IN final return (recorded); FI p7 via Gilbert PDF

epoch2.label: Kota Bharu PSR reacquires outbound (2-minute overhead gap)
FI: 01:39:59 (UTC+8) heading 244M, ground speed 529 kt, 'height at 32,800 ft' (military; disclaimed)
epoch2.source: ATC-Caption-2025 KB_PSR_OUT (recorded); FI p3 altitude quote

epoch2.label: Last Kota Bharu PSR return, crossing the peninsula toward Penang
Butterworth civil primary radar acquires the target
Butterworth track (data gap ~01:48 (UTC+8)-01:51 (UTC+8) follows)
Approaching Penang Island from the northeast
South of Penang Island (FI: Kota Bharu-captured target P3426 dropped 01:52:35 (UTC+8) ~6 NM south of Penang; SIR quotes a disclaimed 44,700 ft here)
epoch2.source: ATC-Caption-2025 BW_PSR corrected (recorded); FI p7/p23, SIR p50

epoch2.label: Right turn onto ~290° up the Strait of Malacca
Last civil primary radar return (Butterworth). SIR: military blip near Pulau Perak at 1801:59 at a disclaimed 4,800 ft
epoch2.source: ATC-Caption-2025 BW_PSR corrected final return (recorded); SIR p50

epoch2.label: Last point of the 10-second military-derived track. SIR: the blip disappeared at 1803:09
epoch2.source: Geoscience/ATSB KL-LSTRP numeric track final 10-s point; SIR p50

epoch2.label: RADAR GAP (SIR: no returns 02:03:09 (UTC+8)-02:15:25 (UTC+8)) - straight-line interpolation toward airway N571
Passing waypoint VAMPI on N571 (FI-timed anchor inside the radar gap)
epoch2.source: time: FI p7 via Gilbert PDF; position: OpenNav VAMPI (6°10'56"N 97°35'08"E)

epoch2.label: Along N571 VAMPI-MEKAR (military radar reacquired 02:15:25 (UTC+8) per SIR; no numeric positions published for this segment)
Passing north of waypoint MEKAR (MEKAR at 6.5039N 96.4911E)
epoch2.source: time: FI p7 via Gilbert PDF; position interpolated ~7 NM past MEKAR along N571 toward NILAM (MEKAR/NILAM from OpenNav)

epoch2.label: Last primary radar fix, 10 NM past MEKAR on N571 toward NILAM. SIR: '195 nm from Butterworth, heading 285°, 516 kt, 29,500 ft' (altitude disclaimed). Corroborated by the KL-LSTRP numeric track's final row 6.5999N 96.3111E (~2.8 km away); Lido-slide alternative 6.871123N 97.354047E not used
epoch2.source: time: FI p7 / SIR p50; position: SIR 10-NM-past-MEKAR interpretation as computed by mh370search (Iannello); cross-check: Geoscience/ATSB KL-LSTRP xlsx final row
